sizing_rule.note: Ralph Vince optimal f method: Position Size = Account Equity / (Largest Historical Loss / Optimal Fraction). The optimal_f parameter must be calculated from historical drawdown analysis. Example shows f=0.1, but this is context-specific and requires empirical derivation from trade series statistics (win rate, avg win, avg loss, standard deviation).

source_rule_text: Size positions based on a fixed fractional approach using the ratio of account equity to the largest loss, scaled by an optimal fraction multiplier.
